# Trump administration moves key student loan oversight to Treasury

Transferring the oversight of defaulted student loans is a significant step toward dismantling the Education Department.

4 min

Make us preferred on Google

The U.S. Department of Education building in D.C. in 2024. (Jose Luis Magana/AP)

By 
Danielle Douglas-Gabriel

The Education Department said Thursday that the Treasury Department will assume responsibility for collecting on defaulted student loans, the first of three phases to spin off key functions of the office of Federal Student Aid.

# Latest Trump DEI Ban for Federal Funds Recipients Sees Pushback

March 24, 2026, 7:42 PM UTC

Tiana Headley

Reporter

Higher education trade groups are pushing back on the Trump administration’s latest move to target diversity, equity, and inclusion among colleges and universities.

A proposal by the General Services Administration would require all federal funding recipients, including higher education institutions, to certify that they don’t have “illegal” DEI programs or practices to maintain access. [...] The American Council on Education, Association of Public and Land-grant Universities, and Council for Christian Colleges & Universities are among almost two dozen organizations that say the proposal “would create significant uncertainty and insecurity for colleges and universities and their efforts to comply with federal law,” ...

news.title: 美教育部启动新一轮教育资助计划
news.summary: 据美国《高等教育内幕》报道，美联邦学生贷款政策出现重要调整。新政策涉及还款计划、利息计算及减免资格等多个方面。此举旨在优化学生贷款管理制度，对美国高等教育财政支持体系具有重要影响。有关情况值得持续关注。
news.content:
In addition to applicant-facing changes, the update will also include a new fraud-detection system aimed at curbing a growing financial aid fraud economy that can cost a college or university millions of dollars every year.

The updates, announced at the 2026 Federal Student Aid Training Conference earlier this month, are the latest in a line of efforts to make the FAFSA process faster and more accessible. Those efforts seem to be working, with experts attributing high rates of FAFSA completion in part to those improvements. As of early January, 33 percent of the high school Class of 2026 had filled out the FAFSA, nearly a 10 percent increase from the Class of 2023 (the most recent to experience a regular Oct. 1 FAFSA opening).

# Treasury Department Begins Taking Over Student Loans As The Education Department Gets Dismantled

Breaking off the student loan operation would mark the biggest step yet in closing the department.

COLLIN BINKLEY

LOADINGERROR LOADING [...] “The Department of Education has issued a dizzying series of rule changes that make it harder for borrowers to figure out what their options are on their federal student loans,” said Kyra Taylor, an attorney at the National Consumer Law Center. She warned that any errors in loan collection would have “devastating effects on families.”

Advertisement

The move is part of Trump’s campaign to shutter the Education Department, an agency he says was overrun by liberal thinking. Only Congress has authority to close the department, but Trump officials are picking it apart through a series of inter-government agreements that relocate the department’s operations to other federal offices. [...] Advertisement

The 17-page agreement outlines a stunning realignment of the nation’s federal student loan programs, which have been overseen by the Education Department since it was created more than 40 years ago.

The agreement “marks an intentional and historic step toward breaking up the Federal education bureaucracy and dramatically improving the administration of Federal student aid programs,” Education Secretary Linda McMahon said in a statement.

news.title: 美国各州加速制定AI教育应用政策
news.summary: 据美国教育媒体报道，美国各州加速推进人工智能教育应用政策制定。多州已发布AI教育指导文件，规范课堂教学中AI工具使用。此举旨在引导AI技术在教育领域规范应用，对美国教育数字化转型具有重要影响。有关情况值得持续关注。

news.title: 美国高等教育机构面临预算调整压力
news.summary: 据美国《高等教育纪事报》报道，美国多州高等教育机构面临预算削减压力。州政府拟减少公立大学拨款，部分高校已启动成本管控措施。此举旨在应对州财政压力，对美国公立高等教育发展具有重要影响。有关情况值得持续关注。
